observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancy of the cash balances between the cashbooks and the ledger to reflect the correct amount of Cash - Local Treasury account in the FS, and furthermore, reconcile the cashbook at least quarterly or as the need arises in order to have an accurate, complete and reliable cash balance at any given time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer together with the Municipal Accountant unable to reconcile the cash balances between the cash books and the ledger.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation Number 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the municipal employees to settle their cash advances on the period within which to settle cash advances as mandated in COA Circular 97-002, thus minimizing the risk of exposing government funds to possible loss or misappropriation. Moreover, we recommended Management to require the employees who have unliquidated cash advances as of year-end that was presented in the table to immediately submit their liquidation reports, otherwise implement item 5.9 of the above circular to cover for their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Recommendation was not fully complied with by management. A huge amount of unliquidated cash advances for various activities were not liquidated as of year- end.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation Number 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications for all the above-mentioned transactions for further evaluation and to avoid suspension in audit. Also, oblige the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims are duly supported with the required basic documents to ensure validity and propriety of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Management failed to attached all the required documents to publicly bidded goods and infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation Number 7 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We strongly recommended that the municipal government facilitate availment of FINDES to reduce cash advances as a way of strengthening internal control on cash, thus avoiding high risk of potential loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Management started to communicate with the LBP.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation Number 8 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to invite observers from both NGO and a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand, in accordance with Sections 13.1 and 13.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to achieve transparency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management. The BAC still unable to invite observers from both NGO and a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation Number 9 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE: • require the concerned implementing offices the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom; • advise the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), all offices in-charge of the implementation together with the Municipal Accountant to determine the total unexpended balances of the completed and the projects which were no longer viable and inform the LCE for possible reappropriation and the latter to recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan for possible reappropriation. Thus, funds could be used fully in accordance with the authorized uses as prescribed in RA Nos. 7171 and 8240; • ensure that only feasible programs/projects/activities were included in the Annual Investment Program; and • require regular monitoring of implementation as a basis in taking appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation was partially complied. Reiterated in Observation Number 10 of this report. Recommendation was partially complied. Reiterated in Observation Number 10 of this report. Recommendation was partially complied. Reiterated in Observation Number 10 of this report. Recommendation was partially complied with by management. Reiterated in Observation Number 10 of this report.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation Number 10 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE enforce the collection of these taxes, and: • require the Municipal Treasurer to exert more efforts to collect from the taxpayers and for those who have been unable to pay their taxes for several years, issue notices of delinquency for them to settle their unpaid taxes; • if circumstances warrant, avail of the Civil remedies provided for by the provisions of the Local Government Code (RA 7160), specifically Sections 174 to 176 thereof. Section 174 provides for the civil remedies which are by (a) administrative action through distraint of personal properties and (b) by judicial actions. Section 175 provides for the procedure on the distraint of personal property and Section 176 provides for the levy of Real Properties.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation was not complied with by management. Reiterated in Observation Number 11 of this report. Recommendation was not complied with by management. Reiterated in Observation Number 11 of this report.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation Number 11 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancy of the
cash balances between the cashbooks and the ledger to reflect the correct
amount of Cash - Local Treasury account in the FS, furthermore,
reconcile the cashbook at least quarterly or as the need arises in order
to have an accurate, complete and reliable cash balance at any given time.

We recommended that the LCE oblige:
2.6.1 the Municipal Treasurer to transfer immediately to the Trust
Fund -Cash in Bank account the total amount due to the fund to
facilitate accounting and monitoring of said fund and avoid
confusion on the use of check to be issued; and
2.6.2 the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary accounting
entries after the bank transfer to reflect the correct balances of
Cash in Bank account for each fund.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Assessor to secure the necessary documents to support the ownership claims of the Municipality over the purchased land and to avoid suspension in audit. Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant to require all the necessary documents before payment shall be made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation was complied with by management. Lacking documents for the purchased land were already submitted to COA Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC and the Supply Officer to strictly adhere to the requirements of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 specifically item B therein. Likewise, advise the BAC to require the dealers/suppliers to indicate the brand names of the items being quoted in the RFQ which will be the basis of the BAC in filling-out of the PO and the Supplier for the issuance of Sales Invoice, thus delivered goods/items could be easily verified and validated.
observations.managementAction: The Audit Team’s recommendation was fully complied with by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the AT list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year as mandated by the aforementioned COA Circular in order that the AT could conduct timely validation and monitoring of the implementation of infrastructure projects and to inform management promptly of any deficiency for the latter’s immediate and appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the Audit Team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to verify the correct amounts of due to BIR or Philhealth in order to remit the exact amounts to avoid over remittances of any statutory obligations, thus the affected accounts will be fairly presented in the financial statements. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to inquire to the BIR on the remedies to recover the overremitted amount as the same was not actually due to the BIR. Moreover, collect from the defaulting employees the overremitted Due to Philhealth as a result of negligence considering Philhealth contributions are consumable which cannot be withdrawn or applied in the succeeding contributions. After which, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the affected account/s.
observations.managementAction: The Audit Team’s recommendation was complied with by management. Recommendation was implemented by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant to compute and withhold taxes from the payment of BAC honoraria as mandated by BIR Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018, and remit the same to the BIR within the prescribed period in order not to deprive the National Government of the additional income that could be utilized for various programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant complied with the Audit Team’s recommendation. Taxes were already withheld from the payment of BAC Honoraria.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the MPDC and Municipal Engineer to intensify the implementation of the PPAs funded out from the 20% DF and to monitor closely the implementation thereof so as to deliver to the constituents, timely benefits that could be derived from these projects and to achieve a broader scope of development. We also recommended management to reappropriate/ realign projects which were not implementable due to some constraints including the remaining amounts of projects already implemented. We further recommended management to program only projects which were attainable and implementable within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Management complied to the Audit Team’s recommendation. Management implemented the Audit Team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: No specific recommendation beyond continued compliance was included in the prior year entry.
observations.managementAction: Management continued to comply on the withholding and remittance of the mandatory contributions required by the national government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the necessary documents detailing the information relative to replaced parts of road networks and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant to serve as her basis in determining the appropriate carrying amount of replaced parts of local road networks to be derecognized from the books in order to present fairly the Road Networks account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Pages 40 to 41 of the 2022 AAR | Recommendation not complied with. Management commented they will finish the inventory by June 2024.
observations.reason: No report was submitted yet to the AT.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Supply/ Property Officer to list down all unserviceable properties, prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and consider the immediate disposal of the same to avoid further deterioration and to realize possible income therefrom, the Supply Officer will be relieved from accountability and Management will have the storage space for other purposes. Thereafter, submit the report to the Municipal Accountant for dropping of the related property accounts from the books of accounts after its disposal.
observations.managementAction: Pages 45 to 46 of the 2022 AAR | Not Complied with. Management is still in the process of accomplishing the required report.
observations.reason: IIRUP for unserviceable properties was not yet prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE designate a personnel in-charge, who can easily be trained to perform the posting requirements or at least possesses a technical background of the task, and consider sending him for training to become knowledgeable in posting the bid opportunities and notices in the agency website. Likewise, we recommended that the procuring entity strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of the RA No. 9184, especially on the posting requirements to ensure transparency and accountability in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction:
Pages 51 to 53 of the 2022 AAR | Recommendation was not complied with.
Management claimed that they are still in the process of scouting an Administrator to handle the website.
observations.reason: Bidding documents, Invitation to bid, Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed were still not posted in the Agency Website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Assessor to secure the necessary documents to support the ownership claims of the Municipality over the purchased land and to avoid suspension in audit. Moreover, instruct to the Municipal Accountant to require all the necessary documents before payment shall be made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.
observations.managementAction:
Pages 57 to 58 of the 2022 AAR | Partially complied.
The Municipal Assessor is still in the process of producing all the required documents.
observations.reason: Only partial documents were secured by the Municipal Assessor. Reiterated in Observation Number 4 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Implementing Unit the promptly implementation of the projects in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom. Moreover, advise the Local Development Council to ensure that only feasible programs/ projects/ activities are included in the Annual Investment Program and require regular monitoring of implementation as basis in taking appropriate action. Thus, the benefits that could be derived from the programs/project would be timely and judiciously extended to the tobacco farmers, as they are the very reason for which the funds were created.
observations.managementAction: Pages 63 to 65 of the 2022 AAR | Recommendation was partially complied with.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation Number 11 of this report.

Moreover, we recommended Management to require the employees who have unliquidated cash advances as of year-end that was presented in the table to immediately submit their liquidation reports, otherwise implement item 5.9 of the above circular to cover for their unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies for all the above-mentioned transactions for further evaluation and to avoid suspension in audit. Likewise, advise the BAC to require the dealers/suppliers to indicate the brand names of the items being quoted in the RFQ which will be the basis of the BAC in filling-out of the PO and the Supplier for the issuance of Sales Invoice, thus delivered goods/items could be easily verified and validated.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Budget Officer the status of on-going infrastructure projects as his basis in recording the obligation and the Municipal Accountant in preparing the necessary journal entry vouchers to record the partial/ unbilled work accomplished and to the transfer the cost of completed projects to the appropriate Infrastructure Assets to present the correct balances of the affected PPE and payable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Pages 38 to 40 of the 2022 AAR | Recommendation was complied with. The List of Statement of Work Accomplished were submitted to the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: The List of Statement of Work Accomplished were submitted to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify all PPE items costing below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00 and close the corresponding accumulated depreciation to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Pages 41 to 43 of the 2022 AAR | AT’s recommendation was complied with.
observations.reason: Validated in the submitted Inventory Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Supply Officer to coordinate with the implementing offices for the submission of the RIS and Certified List of Recipients to the Municipal Accountant as her basis in recording the Inventory Expense. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to reconcile their records and adjust based on valid documents in order to present the correct balances of the inventory of accounts and related accounts.
observations.managementAction: Pages 43 to 45 of the 2022 AAR | Recommendation was complied with.
observations.reason: Validated in the submitted Inventory Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation of the PPE accounts to present fairly the financial condition and performance of the LGU in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Pages 47 to 48 of the 2022 AAR | Recommendation was complied with.
observations.reason: Validated in the review of financial statements

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly enforce direct payments to suppliers/ dealers for the procurement of goods in observance of sound internal control on the disbursement process to ensure the validity of the transactions and in order to withhold the amount of taxes due to the government as required in the above-cited revenue regulations.
observations.managementAction: Pages 48 to 49 of the 2022 AAR | Recommendation was complied with.
observations.reason: Validated during post-audit of disbursement of vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders with their corresponding supporting documents to the Auditor within five (5) working days from execution and issuance thereof for review and evaluation so that management will be immediately informed of any defects and deficiencies noted and to facilitate the inspection of completed projects and delivered of goods.
observations.managementAction: Pages 49 to 51 of the 2022 AAR | Recommendation was complied with.
observations.reason: Validated in the management’s submitted reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Department Heads concerned to formulate their respective PPMPs showing the specific procurement program/project to be implemented and provide the necessary information needed to facilitate the consolidation of the same into APP to ensure transparency and provide a good basis for monitoring the implementation of the procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: Pages 54 to 56 of the 2022 AAR | Recommendation was complied with.
observations.reason: PPMPs of the different departments were prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to subject all procurement to public bidding unless the conditions are present for the use of alternative methods upon approval of the LCE to ensure that the most advantageous price and terms for the LGU is obtained.
observations.managementAction: Pages 56 to 57 of the 2022 AAR | Recommendation was complied with.
observations.reason: Validated in the post-audit of disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies observed for further evaluation of the transactions and to avoid suspension in audit. Also, oblige the Municipal Accountant to see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents to ensure their validity and propriety.
observations.managementAction: Pages 58 to 63 of the 2022 AAR | Recommendation was complied with.
observations.reason: Lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies noted for CY 2022 transactions were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Engineering Office to conduct all the necessary detailed engineering activities before the conduct of procurement process to avoid the issuance of suspension orders and ensure the timely completion of the projects. Issue only suspension order when necessary to avoid delay in the implementation of infrastructure projects. Thus, the timely implementation and completion of the projects and the supposed beneficiaries would enjoy the benefits that could have been derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction:
Pages 65 to 67 of the 2022 AAR | Recommendation was complied with.
The three infrastructure projects were already implemented during the year.
observations.reason: Validated during the post-audit of disbursement vouchers for infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the General Services Officer (GSO) to list down all unserviceable properties, prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and consider the immediate disposal of the same to avoid further deterioration and to realize possible income therefrom. Thereafter, submit the report to the Municipal Accountant for dropping of the related property accounts from the books of accounts after its disposal.
observations.managementAction: The GSO prepared the IIRUP for submission to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
To establish the reliability of the reported Inventories and corresponding expense accounts, we recommended that the LCE:

Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories every year and submit the corresponding report to the Auditor not later January 31 for verification.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) observe the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting for inventories; (b) maintain supplies ledger cards; and (c) reconcile regularly with the records maintained by the Property/ Supply Office.

Require the Property/ Supply Officer to (a) maintain stock cards; (b) prepare and submit monthly RSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to the Municipal Accountant as basis in recording the issuances of inventory items; and (c) regularly reconcile with the inventory records maintained by the Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction: RPCI was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents to facilitate the technical evaluation of the infrastructure projects and goods procured and to avoid suspension in audit. Also, oblige the Municipal Accountant to make sure that all necessary documents pertaining to the procurement of goods and implementation of infrastructrure projects are attached to the disbursement voucher before payment is made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer was able to submit some of the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to adopt the required procedures and conditions in the Post-Qualification stage particularly in the verification, validation and ascertainment of all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the LCB to ensure that such documents are in accordance with the requirements as stated in the Bidding Documents, thus eligible bidders would ultimately be awarded the contract.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders with their corresponding supporting documents to the Auditor within five working days from execution and issuance thereof for review and evaluation so that management will be immediately informed of any defects and deficiencies noted and to facilitate the inspection of completed projects and delivered of goods.
observations.managementAction: Notices of Award, Notices to Proceed and copies of contracts were posted in the PhilGEPS website.
observations.reason: Posting of some Notices of Award was not made with three calendar days from issuance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to strictly observe the posting of requests for price quotation/ proposal and the notices of award together with the contract or purchase order for procurement of goods and services through alternative methods of procurement in the PhilGEPS website and at any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality to ensure interested users’ ready access to information to promote transparency and competition in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to submit the quantified accomplishment reports to determine the urgency and necessity of the rendered overtime services, hence, any deficiencies will be communicated to Management for immediate and appropriate action. Also, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that future transactions are supported with complete and proper documentation to establish their validity and propriety. 
We also recommended that Management formulate internal rules and policies on the rendition of overtime services, which shall be circumscribed with the policies and procedures under CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 dated November 25, 2015 to avoid the incurrence of irregular expenditures particularly on the granting of overtime pay to the department heads. Except for the entitlement to overtime pay, they are however not precluded from rendering overtime services whenever the exigency of the service requires.
observations.managementAction: The required supporting documents were not submitted yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other implementing offices to review the composition of the 20% DF, identify the savings from fully implemented projects and those which are no longer prioritized and, subject to the provisions of the Local Government Code and the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, reappropriate to current/ existing priority development projects. Moreover, Management have to ensure that the fund will not be used for any purpose beyond those expressly prescribed therein in order to attain the purposes for which the fund was created.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to closely monitor the implementation of the programs and projects under the 20% DF to attain desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes of the Municipality and the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The amount of ₱8,980,206.72 was utilized during the year.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Government was not able to implement projects with appropriation amounting to ₱2,854,600.70 which represents 24 percent of the total amount of P12,019,081.60.

Reiterated in Observation No. 9, page 59 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate completion/ submission of the requisite documents for the immediate settlement of the audit suspensions as prescribed in the RRSA under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid before effecting payment in order to prevent the same from being suspended/  disallowed in post-audit.
observations.managementAction: Suspensions totaling P77,243,282.07 were settled in CY 2021 and during the 1st and 2nd quarters of CY 2022.
observations.reason: Six Notices of Suspension were not yet settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to update the Municipal Accountant on the status of infrastructure projects and forward the necessary documents to ensure that completed projects which remained not paid as of year-end are recognized in the books of accounts pursuant to Section 350 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the DVs and strictly observe the timely submission of transaction documents to enable the Auditor to conduct timely audit, thus, deficiencies, if any, will be immediately communicated to management for prompt and appropriate action and to avoid the imposition of sanctions as provided under Section 122 (2) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC to adopt the required procedures and conditions in the Post-Qualification stage particularly in the verification, validation and ascertainment of all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the LCB to ensure that they are in accordance with the requirements as stated in the Bidding Documents, thus eligible bidders would ultimately be awarded the contract.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents to facilitate the technical evaluation of the infrastructure projects and goods and to avoid supension of these transactions in audit. Oblige the  Municipal Accountant to make sure that all necessary documents pertaining to the procurement of goods and implementation of infrastructrure projects are attached to the disbursement voucher before payment is made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Assessor to secure the necessary documents to support the ownership claims of the Municipality over the purchased land. Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant to require all the necessary documents before payment shall be made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies observed for further evaluation of the transactions and to avoid suspension in audit. Also, oblige the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents to warrant their validity and propriety.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to look into each CAFOA if it is completely accomplished before a particular transaction is processed and record the obligation into the appropriate RAAOs to ensure that it does not exceed the available allotment. Moreover, require her to reconcile regularly with the records maintained by the Municipal Budget Officer.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders with their corresponding supporting documents to the Auditor within five working days from execution and issuance thereof for review and evaluation so that management will be immediately informed of any defects and deficiencies noted and to facilitate the inspection of completed projects and delivered of goods.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other concerned offices to coordinate and strategize the implementation of all the programs/projects embodied under the 20% DF to attain desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the Municipality and intended beneficiaries.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the LCPC Focal Person to monitor the implementation of PPAs funded under the one percent internal revenue allotment geared towards the promotion and protection of the interest and welfare of children.

We recommended and Management agreed require the GAD Focal Person to formulate appropriate PPAs amidst the COVID-19 pandemic in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the GSO to assign a knowledgeable personnel to check the details or review the propriety and completeness of information on the trip tickets and to regularly submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor thru the Municipal Accountant for purposes of verification on the reasonableness of fuel consumption and Management will be informed of any deficiencies for its immediate and appropriate action. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement vouchers pertaining to payment of fuel, lubricants and oil are duly supported with the required documents in order to ascertain the accuracy, propriety and reasonableness of the expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to comply with the posting requirement of the Approved Bidding Documents to ensure timely dissemination of the procurement opportunity to provide prospective bidders ample time to examine the said documents and to prepare their respective bid proposal.
observations.managementAction: Approved bidding documents were posted in the PhilGEPS website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies noted in support of the validity and propriety of the transactions and to avoid suspension in audit. Instruct the MSWDO to review and verify carefully the completeness and authenticity of the eligibility requirements of the proposed beneficiaries to ensure that the social amelioration programs of the national government are provided to the qualified individuals. Moreover, see to it that the beneficiaries as listed in the payroll are based on the DSWD-generated list so as not to deprive the identified beneficiaries of the needed assistance.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to comply with the submission of the Final APP to the GPPB on or before the end of January of the budget year and post the same in the GPPB website to ensure transparency in the procurement process and in the implementation of procurement contracts.
observations.managementAction: APP for CY 2022 was submitted to the GPPB on January 13, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to observe closer monitoring and evaluation of all ongoing infrastructure projects to ensure the completion of the projects within the specified contract duration. Moreover, instruct the Municipal Engineer to implement the calibrated measures for contracts with negative slippage during the contract duration as provided under GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 dated March 8, 2019 to ensure the timely implementation of the infrastructure projects. Lastly, oblige the Municipal Accountant to send a demand letter to the contractor and require him to refund the overpayment of contract attributed to liquidated damage to avoid issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to comply with the audit recommendation.

Technical Specifications were already included in the detailed engineering design and drawings for projects for CY 2020.

The recommendation was implemented by the concerned employees.












The CSHP was integrated in construction projects for CY 2020.





Submission of performance security was required from the winning bidder.





The BAC Secretariat exerted their best effort to post the required documents in the PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies noted to facilitate the evaluation and inspection of the completed projects and procured goods and to avoid suspension in audit. 

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant require all the necessary documents pertaining to the implementation of infrastructure projects and procurement of goods before payment shall be made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC Secretariat was instructed to compile the lacking documents and submit the same to the Audit Team.





The required documents were submitted together with the vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly enforce direct payments to suppliers/dealers in the procurement of goods in observance of sound internal control on the disbursement process and in order to withhold the amount of taxes due to the government as required in the above-cited revenue regulations.
observations.managementAction: Corresponding Value Added Tax/ Expanded Withholding Tax on purchases was deducted from the reimbursed amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders with their supporting documents to the Auditor within five working days from execution and issuance thereof for review and evaluation so that management will be immediately informed of defects and deficiencies noted, if any, and to facilitate the inspection of completed projects and deliveries of goods.
observations.managementAction: Copies of perfected contracts were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to integrate in the budget proposals the allocation of one percent of the IRA for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities intended for the welfare and protection of children as envisioned under RA No. 9344.
observations.managementAction: For compliance in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management enforce the immediate settlement of the audit suspensions as prescribed in the RRSA under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.


Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the same from being suspended/ disallowed in post-audit.
observations.managementAction:
Management submitted the necessary documents as required in the NS.




The Municipal Accountant ensures that the transactions are supported with necessary supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the General Services Officer to list down all unserviceable properties, prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and consider the immediate disposal of the same to avoid further deterioration and to realize possible income therefrom. Thereafter, submit the report to the Municipal Accountant for dropping of the related property accounts from the books of accounts after its disposal.

To establish the reliability of the reported Inventories and corresponding expense accounts, we recommended that the LCE:
Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories every year and submit the corresponding report to the Auditor not later January 31 for verification.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) observe the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting for inventories; (b) maintain supplies ledger cards; and (c) reconcile regularly with the records maintained by the Property/Supply Office.
Require the Property/Supply Officer to (a) maintain stock cards; (b) prepare and submit monthly RSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to the Municipal Accountant as basis in recording the issuances of inventory items; and (c) regularly reconcile with the inventory records maintained by the Accounting Office.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents to facilitate the technical evaluation of the infrastructure projects and goods procured and to avoid suspension in audit. Also, oblige the Municipal Accountant to make sure that all necessary documents pertaining to the implementation of infrastructure projects and procurement of goods shall be attached to the disbursement voucher before payment is made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the General Services Officer to assign a knowledgeable personnel to check the details or review the propriety and completeness of information on the trip tickets and  to regularly submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor thru the Municipal Accountant for purposes of verification on the reasonableness of fuel consumption and Management will be informed of any deficiencies for its immediate and appropriate action. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement vouchers pertaining to payment of fuel, lubricants and oil are duly supported with the required documents in order to ascertain the accuracy, propriety and reasonableness of the expenditures.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to comply with the posting requirement of the Approved Bidding Documents to ensure timely dissemination of the procurement opportunity to provide prospective bidders ample time to examine the said documents and to prepare their respective bid proposal.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to adopt the required procedures and conditions in the Post-Qualification stage particularly in the verification, validation and ascertainment of all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the LCB to ensure that such documents are in accordance with the requirements as stated in the Bidding Documents, thus eligible bidders would ultimately be awarded the contract.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC, through its Secretariat, see to it that the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and a copy of the approved contract are posted in the PhilGEPS website within three and fifteen calendar days from their respective issuances for information and  transparency in the procurement process.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to strictly observe the posting of requests for price quotation/proposal and the notices of award together with the contract or purchase order for procurement of goods and services through alternative methods of procurement in the PhilGEPS website and at any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality to ensure interested users’ ready access to information to promote transparency and competition in the procurement process.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies noted in support of the validity and propriety of the transactions and to avoid suspension in audit. Instruct the MSWDO to review and verify carefully the completeness and authenticity of the eligibility requirements of the proposed beneficiaries to ensure that the social amelioration programs of the national government are provided to the qualified individuals. Moreover, see to it that the beneficiaries as listed in the payroll are based on the DSWD-generated list so as not to deprive the identified beneficiaries of the needed assistance.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to comply with the submission of the Final APP to the GPPB on or before the end of January of the budget year and post the same in the GPPB website to ensure transparency in the procurement process and in the implementation of procurement contracts.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to observe closer monitoring and evaluation of all ongoing infrastructure projects to ensure the completion of the projects within the specified contract duration. Moreover, instruct the Municipal Engineer to implement the calibrated measures for contracts with negative slippage during the contract duration as provided under GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 dated March 8, 2019 to ensure the timely implementation of the infrastructure projects. Lastly, oblige the Municipal Accountant to send a demand letter to the contractor and require him to refund the overpayment of contract attributed to liquidated damage to avoid issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to submit the quantified accomplishment reports to determine the urgency and necessity of the rendered overtime services, hence, any deficiencies will be communicated to Management for immediate and appropriate action. Also, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that future transactions are supported with complete and proper documentation to establish their validity and propriety.
We also recommended that Management formulate internal rules and policies on the rendition of overtime services, which shall be circumscribed with the policies and procedures under CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 dated November 25, 2015 to avoid the incurrence of irregular expenditures particularly on the granting of overtime pay to the department heads. Except for the entitlement to overtime pay, they are however not precluded from rendering overtime services whenever the exigency of the service requires.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other implementing offices to review the composition of the 20% DF, identify the savings from fully implemented projects and those which are no longer prioritized and, subject to the provisions of the Local Government Code and the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, reappropriate to current/existing priority development projects. Moreover, Management have to ensure that the fund will not be used for any purpose beyond those expressly prescribed therein in order to attain the purposes for which the fund was created.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to closely monitor the implementation of the programs and projects under the 20% DF to attain desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes of the Municipality and the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate completion/submission of the requisite documents for the immediate settlement of the audit suspensions as prescribed in the RRSA under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid before effecting payment in order to prevent the same from being suspended/disallowed in post-audit.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the transfer of the costs of completed projects to present the correct balances of the Construction in Progress-Infrastructure Assets and related Infrastructure Assets accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher for the transfer of the costs of the completed projects to the related Infrastructure Asset accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the concerned suppliers to post a warranty security which shall be covered by either retention money in an amount equivalent to at least one percent of every progress payment, or a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent of the total contract to guarantee the performance of their responsibilities in case of manufacturing defects after acceptance of the delivered goods.
observations.managementAction: Retention money was deducted from the payment of contracts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management secure valid official receipts for the abovementioned transactions to properly substantiate the payments made to the contractors. 

We likewise recommended that the Municipal Treasurer thoroughly examine the contents/details in official receipts issued by suppliers/contractors before accepting the same so as not to promote unlawful practices of businesses which intend to defraud the government in terms of taxes that should be paid by them.
observations.managementAction:
Concerned contractors issued a valid official receipts as replacement to the expired ones.


Issued official receipts were properly examined to ascertain their validity.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit a written justification on the non-implementation of the programs/projects in accordance with the scheduled implementation period. 

Moreover, we recommended the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax and reappropriate the savings from the previous years’ implemented programs/projects in accordance with the authorized uses as prescribed in RA Nos. 7171 and 8240 in order to provide the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction:
Management submitted a letter stating therein their comment to the AOM and their justification on the non-implementation of the projects.

For implementation in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate in the LGU’s Annual Budget one percent (1%) of its IRA for the LCPC and allocate the fund for programs, projects and activities for the protection of children.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government will be allocating the required appropriation for LCPC starting CY 2020.
observations.reason: Programs/ projects/ activities for LCPC in CY 2019 were attributed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the transfer of the costs of completed projects to present the correct balances of the Construction in Progress-Infrastructure Assets and related Infrastructure Assets accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned suppliers to post a warranty security which shall be covered by either retention money in an amount equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of every progress payment, or a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of the total contract to guarantee the performance of their responsibilities in case of manufacturing defects after acceptance of the delivered goods.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the deficiencies noted to facilitate the evaluation and inspection of the completed projects and procured goods and to avoid supension in audit. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountaant require all the necessary documents pertaining to the implementation of infrastructrure projects and procurement of goods before payment shall be made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.

We recommended that Management strictly enforce direct payments to suppliers/dealers in the procurement of goods in observance of sound internal control on the disbursement process and in order to withhold the amount of taxes due to the government as required in the above-cited revenue regulations.

We recommended that the Management secure valid official receipts for the abovementioned transactions to properly substantiate the payments made to the contractors. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Treasurer thoroughly examine the contents/details in official receipts issued by suppliers/contractors before accepting the same so as not to promote unlawful practices of businesses which intend to defraud the government in terms of taxes that should be paid by them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders with their supporting documents to the Auditor within five (5) working days from execution and issuance thereof for review and evaluation so that management will be immediately informed of defects and deficiencies noted, if any,  and to facilitate the inspection of completed projects and deliveries of goods.

We recommended that Management submit a written justification on the non-implementation of the programs/projects in accordance with the scheduled implementation period. Moreover, we recommended the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax and reappropriate the savings from the previous years’ implemented programs/projects in accordance with the authorized uses as prescribed in Republic Act Nos. 7171 and 8240 in order to provide the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to integrate in the budget proposals the allocation of one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities intended for the welfare and protection of children as envisioned under Republic Act No. 9344.

We recommended that Management enforce the immediate settlement of the audit suspensions as prescribed in the RRSA under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the same from being suspended/ disallowed in post-audit.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman who is also the Municipal Engineer require the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Auditor a copy of the contracts and all supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted contracts and supporting documents of infrastructure projects and goods for CY 2018.
observations.reason: Contracts and supporting documents were only submitted upon the submission of the corresponding disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents to ensure their legality, validity and propriety.
observations.managementAction: Most of the lacking documents, especially those that were subjected to a Notice of Suspension, were already submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Transactions covering the payments of contracts for the implementation of infrastructure projects were still not supported with the requisite documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management properly plan the implementation of the programs/ projects/ activities under the 20% Development Fund so that the accomplishments and benefits that are expected to be derived therefrom are obtained/ achieved on time.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council formulate priority programs/ projects/ activities funded under the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to attain the purposes for which the fund is intended for.
observations.managementAction: The programs/projects/activities under the 20% Development Fund were implemented in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the prompt implementation of all projects/ programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The unimplemented projects under the RA 7171 will be realigned to other projects.
observations.reason: Some of the unimplemented projects were subject of various issues, such as road right of way and site issue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that upon completion of any project funded from any national government agency, the Municipal Treasurer should return the unused/ unexpended balance to the source agency or remit to the Bureau of Treasury. This will enable the concerned agencies to use the funds for their programs/ projects.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balances for fully implemented projects in CY 2018 were already remitted to the source agency or the Bureau of Treasury during CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit/ release the shares on RPT, SET and other taxes to the concerned LGUs so that the latter may avail of the immediate use of the fund for their programs/ projects/ activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman who is also the Municipal Engineer require the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Auditor a copy of the contracts and all supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution thereof.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents to ensure their legality, validity and propriety.

We recommended that management properly plan the implementation of the programs/projects/activities under the 20% Development Fund so that the accomplishments and benefits that are expected to be derived therefrom are obtained/achieved on time.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council formulate priority programs/projects/activities funded under the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to attain the purposes for which the fund is intended for.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate in the LGU’s Annual Budget one percent (1%) of its IRA for the LCPC and allocate the fund for programs, projects and activities for the protection of children.

We recommended that upon completion of any project funded from any national government agency, the Municipal Treasurer should return the unused/unexpended balance to the source agency or remit to the Bureau of Treasury. This will enable the concerned agencies to use the funds for their programs/projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit/release the shares on RPT, SET and other taxes to the concerned LGUs so that the latter may avail of the immediate use of the fund for their programs/projects/activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the same from being suspended/ disallowed in post-audit.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to follow up the titling of all the parcels of land acquired by the Municipality as required under Section 449, Volume I of GAAM and Section 39(2) of PD 1445 to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor certified that he filed the Application for Titles on all properties owned by the Municipality to the Department of Environment and Natural Resources, thru the DENR Provincial Office, and approval of said application is on process.
observations.reason: Parcels of land were not titled.

observations.recommendation: We   have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify the collection of Real Property and Special Education Taxes.  Continue the issuance of Notices of collection to the delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their obligation to pay their taxes. Likewise, conduct massive tax campaign in all the barangays and offer tax amnesty in order to motivate delinquent taxpayers to avail of the offer.  In addition, seek the cooperation of the barangay officials, anyway, the barangays have also shares in the collection of real property taxes.
observations.managementAction: Conducted tax campaign to increase collection of real property taxes.
observations.reason: Collection efficiency on real property and special education tax was increased by 34.20% due to the payment  received from the NPC (National Grid Corporation) covering CYs 2010 to 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned exert extra effort to collect such receivables so that the fund could be used by other deserving individuals.  If such efforts exerted are futile, the Municipal Mayor may file the request for authority to write –off dormant accounts to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor.  The request shall be supported by complete documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: No action taken because there are debtors who are already dead and the whereabouts of some are unknown.
observations.reason: No collection on KKK loan receivables during the year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement programs/ projects funded under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the prompt implementation of all funds received from other national government agencies in order to deliver on time the benefits due to the intended beneficiaries and to avoid returning the unutilized funds to the donor agencies.
observations.managementAction: All the projects were implemented during the year except for the fund received from Sen. N. Binay.
observations.reason: Only one project was not implemented, re: Rehabilitation Assistance from Sen. N. Binay in the amount of P30,000.00.
